spec_match.rationale: The page now tests the exact soft-navigation contract it claims to explain: supportedEntryTypes, PerformanceSoftNavigationTiming exposure, observer subscription eligibility, and live or simulated per-route entries.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified Home/Docs/Blog/About route controls and Clear soft. Route clicks update the visible page, URL, soft count, last observed entry, and timeline entries; Clear resets the soft timeline.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now separates unsupported browser state from runtime failure, avoids subscribing when soft-navigation is not advertised, keeps a simulated fallback for unsupported browsers, and fixes the hard-navigation duration and route accumulation edge cases.

references.rationale: The see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5144837209194496 and the WICG Soft Navigations spec.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP reproduced the original issues: missing ChromeStatus link, a hard navigation duration rendered as a huge negative value, and SPA history URLs accumulating route segments that broke relative see-also links after pushState. After repair, DevTools MCP verified a clean initial unsupported-state probe, exact contract readouts for supportedEntryTypes, constructor exposure, observer subscription, and last observed entry; Docs and Blog route clicks use stable ?route= URLs, increment soft navigation timeline entries, and keep absolute related links correct; Clear soft resets the counter and last observed entry. The concept page and updated conformance route both had clean consoles and successful local requests; conformance reported 6 PASS / 0 FAIL.
